title: /commit and /commit-push-pr inline the same git pipeline twice (DRY)
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: DRY / KISS
Location: src/cli/repl/slash.rs:846-944 (/commit arm) and :946-~1100 (/commit-push-pr arm). Both open 'use std::process::Command' inside the function, re-check 'rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree', re-run 'diff --cached --stat' and 'diff --stat', re-run the stage-all prompt, re-run the commit with default or edited message. /commit-push-pr additionally runs 'push' and 'gh pr create'.
Violation: The /commit-push-pr arm literally copy-pastes the commit logic then appends push. Bug fixes to one arm will drift from the other. 'use std::process::Command' is declared twice (846, 947) inside function scopes — a lint smell.
Mandated Refactor: Extract fn git_commit_flow(target: CommitTarget) -> Result<CommitOutcome> where CommitTarget = CommitOnly | CommitAndPush | CommitPushPr. Or better: extract a GitGateway trait with commit(), push(), open_pr() methods and compose them. Commit-push-pr becomes: let oid = gateway.commit(msg)?; gateway.push("origin", branch)?; gateway.open_pr(title, body)?; — three lines instead of ~250.
